title: Runtime component introspection across React Fiber and Vue VNode

fields.description: PinFlow's pixel-to-code workflow needs a unified runtime adapter that introspects component trees from a running web application. Today the system supports React (via Fiber tree walking) and Vue 3 (via VNode introspection). Both APIs are framework-internal, change across minor versions, and must yield stable element IDs across HMR cycles to power source-location mapping for coding agents over MCP. We are looking for libraries that solve framework-agnostic component introspection, fiber/vnode walkers, devtools-protocol bridges, or runtime source-mapping helpers — so we can borrow patterns instead of maintaining brittle hand-written adapters.
fields.current_approach:
- React adapter walks the Fiber tree via `__REACT_DEVTOOLS_GLOBAL_HOOK__` and a custom commit-renderer interceptor
- Vue adapter walks the VNode tree via `__VUE_HMR_RUNTIME__` and component-instance introspection
- Element IDs are content-hashed from props + source location
- Both adapters live in `packages/runtime-adapter-react` and `packages/runtime-adapter-vue` and share types via `packages/protocol`

The current pain: each framework's internal API drifts across minor versions, so the adapter packages need maintenance pressure. We want to know whether the open-source community has a more robust pattern.
success_criteria: A reusable adapter pattern that exposes a uniform component-introspection API for React 18+ and Vue 3+
Stable element IDs that survive hot module replacement at >80% cache hit rate
Zero production runtime cost (instrumentation must strip out cleanly in production builds)
Source-location mapping (file path, line, column, component name) available per element
constraints: No coupling to a specific bundler beyond Vite/Webpack
No reliance on production-stripped framework hooks
Must work in dev-only mode with explicit feature flags
non_goals: Supporting Angular or Svelte adapters (out of current scope)
Building a full standalone DevTools UI
Replacing the React DevTools or Vue DevTools browser extensions
